channel.title: summarizedStoriesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is extended by 3 weeks as tensions rise in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Hezbollah and Israel traded fire just hours after the ceasefire extension was announced, underscoring its fragility.

item.title: 'Self-aware' robots can learn complex tasks by watching humans. Is that a good thing?
item.description: Scientists say they've made a key breakthrough that would allow robots to figure out complex tasks on their own, but experts say it raises questions a...

item.title: Ignoring the War Has Been Working for Long-Term Investors
item.description: The U.S. stock market’s quick rebound this month may be fragile, but riding out the market’s disconcerting waves has been a sound strategy.

item.title: Tariffs Raised Consumers’ Prices, but the Refunds Go Only to Businesses
item.description: Many families felt the sting of the president’s now-illegal tariffs, but companies have said little about whether they will share the $166 billion com...

item.title: How Pittsburgh — host of this year's NFL draft — became a sports mecca
item.description: The Pennsylvania city is hosting the draft for the first time in almost 80 years. Pittsburghers say the city's passionate fanbases and winning teams m...

item.title: Mamdani Creates Office to Fight Deed Theft in New York City
item.description: The office will seek to crack down on the practice, in which people fraudulently take ownership of others’ homes.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire. And, Trump eases medical marijuana rules
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks, President Trump says. And, the Trump administration is easing rules on medic...

item.title: Thousands of seafarers stranded by ongoing U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.

item.title: Mamdani Expected to Reject Bill Involving Police at School Protests
item.description: The bill, which is on track to receive Zohran Mamdani’s first veto as mayor, would compel the Police Department to release plans on how it intends to ...

item.title: Why Everyone, Including Trump, Is Talking About Airline Mergers
item.description: A few carriers are making a lot of money while many are struggling. President Trump and industry executives seem to think mergers may be the answer.

item.title: Europeans Ask: What Would It Mean to Defend One Another Without NATO?
item.description: European Union nations have a little-known obligation to protect one another. Experts caution it is no replacement for NATO.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Blockade Will Last ‘as Long as It Takes,’ Hegseth Says
item.description: Iran has made lifting the blockade of its ports a condition of resuming talks to end the war. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that U.S. forces had...

item.title: Trump Extends Waiver Allowing Foreign Ships to Move Goods Between U.S. Ports
item.description: The move was intended to ensure the flow of fuel in the United States, but some economists say it might reduce gas prices for consumers only a small a...

item.title: Why Everyone, Including Trump, Is Talking About Airline Mergers
item.description: A few carriers are making a lot of money while many are struggling. President Trump and industry executives seem to think mergers may be the answer.
